title: Static web skeleton + privacy CI gates (no telemetry, no search logging, CSP + runtime egress E2E)
context: User water-system queries reveal location and a health concern and must never be logged. The web app is static-first — no app server, so no server-side query log is architecturally possible by design. Privacy must be enforced at three layers: a strict Content Security Policy, a runtime network-interception E2E test that fails on any unexpected outbound request, and a static CI audit (no analytics, no trackers, no PII fields, no server-side search log). A grep alone does not constitute passing privacy CI.
objective: Build the static web skeleton (app/) that: (1) loads without errors in a browser; (2) passes TypeScript/ESM lint and build; (3) enforces strict CSP with locked connect-src, script-src, img-src, and font-src 'self'; (4) passes a runtime network-interception E2E test (Playwright or equivalent) that fails on any unexpected outbound request; (5) passes a static CI audit confirming no analytics, no trackers, no PII fields, and no server-side search log; (6) has no telemetry and no third-party scripts; (7) meets initial WCAG 2.2 AA structure requirements.
acceptanceCriteria: Static site loads in a browser without errors
No analytics, trackers, third-party scripts, or PII fields present — verified by static CI audit (not grep alone)
No server-side search log is possible by design (static hosting, no backend)
CSP is locked: connect-src, script-src, img-src, and font-src 'self' enforced
Runtime network-interception E2E test (Playwright) fails the build on any unexpected outbound request during any user flow
TypeScript, ESM; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass
output: PR adding app/ (static site skeleton, no-telemetry/no-PII), CI configs for CSP + runtime egress E2E and static audit; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass.
